text.title: Pattern Selection Guide and Modern Alternatives
text.summary: Scenario-to-pattern mapping, modern language alternatives that have superseded some GoF patterns, and AI-influenced patterns emerging in agent-based systems.

rule.statement: Select a pattern by the problem it solves, not by its name. Identify the exact mismatch or design pressure first, then map it to a pattern. Never apply a pattern because it sounds appropriate.
rule.rationale: Pattern misapplication is more harmful than no pattern: a Singleton used for "shared state" introduces global mutable state; a Facade used as a catch-all creates a God Object.

list.item: "Integrate legacy/third-party API with incompatible interface" → Adapter
"Add behavior (logging, caching) without subclassing" → Decorator
"Simplify complex subsystem for callers" → Facade
"Control access: lazy loading, auth, caching proxy" → Proxy
"Tree structure with uniform leaf/branch treatment" → Composite
"Swap algorithm at runtime" → Strategy
"React to state changes across multiple objects" → Observer
"Encapsulate request for undo/redo/queuing" → Command
"Behavior changes as object moves through states" → State
"Distributed transaction across services" → Saga
"Atomic DB write + message publish" → Outbox
"Independent read/write scaling" → CQRS
"Prevent cascade failure to failing downstream" → Circuit Breaker
Strategy → first-class functions or lambdas (Python, JS, Go); pass behavior as a function parameter
Command → async job queue with a serialized payload (Celery, BullMQ, Temporal); Command object = queue message
Observer → native event emitters (Node.js EventEmitter, Python asyncio events, RxJS Observables)
Iterator → generator functions (Python yield, JS function*, Go channel range); no Iterator class needed
Singleton → DI container scoped as singleton (FastAPI Depends with singleton scope, Spring @Bean, NestJS providers)
Factory → DI container resolves concrete types; explicit Factory only needed for runtime-determined types
Template Method → function composition in FP languages; higher-order functions pass the varying steps
RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation): Repository pattern for vector search + Facade hiding embedding/retrieval/rerank — a Facade + Repository combination
Agent Tool Use: Command pattern — each tool is a Command with an execute() method; the agent is the Invoker
Guardrail: Chain of Responsibility — a sequence of validation handlers applied to every LLM input/output
Model Router: Strategy — select the model (fast/cheap vs capable/expensive) based on query classification at runtime
